questions.objective_ref: Explain in plain words what it means for a model to be well-calibrated.
Describe how a model can attach a confidence number to a prediction.
Read a reliability diagram and say whether a model is over- or under-confident.
Tell the difference between a model being accurate and a model being calibrated.
Explain why a confidence score is only useful for decisions when the model is calibrated.
Tell the difference between a model being accurate and a model being calibrated (calibration is a group property, not a single-prediction one).
